curatedFacts.founded: 1846, Canada's first capital (1841-1844), known as 'The Limestone City'
curatedFacts.landmarks: Fort Henry (UNESCO), Kingston City Hall, Royal Military College, Thousand Islands, Martello Towers, Kingston Penitentiary
curatedFacts.notable: known as 'The Limestone City' — Canada's first capital, gateway to the Thousand Islands and Rideau Canal UNESCO World Heritage Site
curatedFacts.economy: education (Queen's University, RMC, St. Lawrence College), healthcare (Kingston Health Sciences Centre), tourism, corrections, military
curatedFacts.entityDesc: Kingston is a city in Eastern Ontario, Ontario, Canada, with a population of approximately 132,485. Education (queen's university, rmc, st. lawrence college), healthcare (kingston health sciences centre), tourism, corrections, military.
curatedFacts.longDesc:
Kingston isn't just Ontario's city — it's a place where ambition meets opportunity. Founded Canada's first capital (1841-1844), known as 'The Limestone City', this city has transformed into a thriving community of over ~132,000 people.

What makes Kingston remarkable for {BUSINESS_TYPE}? Character and community. The city encompasses everything from vibrant downtown waterfront, Princess Street shopping, Fort Henry Guard ceremonies, 1000 Islands Cruise, Wolfe Island ferry. Each neighborhood has its own character, its own needs, and its own expectations.

The local economy is driven by education (Queen's University, RMC, St. Lawrence College), healthcare (Kingston Health Sciences Centre), tourism, corrections, military. This economic foundation creates steady demand for services and supports a community that takes pride in maintaining its properties and infrastructure.

Community features include Fort Henry (UNESCO), Kingston City Hall, Royal Military College, Thousand Islands, Martello Towers, Kingston Penitentiary, which give Kingston its distinctive character.

Kingston benefits from its location in Eastern Ontario. Whether you need routine {SERVICE} maintenance or a major project, {COMPANY_NAME} understands what Kingston properties need. We've invested the time to learn this community — the local building codes, the weather patterns, the neighbourhood expectations. That's why Kingston residents keep choosing {COMPANY_NAME}.

Our promise: transparent pricing, professional execution, results that last. Call {PHONE} for your {ESTIMATE_TYPE} estimate today.

curatedFacts.founded: 1878, named after Lady Arabella Gore, wife of Lieutenant Governor Francis Gore
curatedFacts.landmarks: Glanmore National Historic Site, Empire Theatre, Belleville City Hall, Zwick's Island, Bay of Quinte
curatedFacts.notable: the 'Friendly City' — located on the Bay of Quinte with strong United Empire Loyalist heritage and proximity to Prince Edward County wine region
curatedFacts.economy: manufacturing, military (CFB Trenton nearby), healthcare, agriculture, food processing (Kellogg's, Parmalat)
curatedFacts.entityDesc: Belleville is a city in Eastern Ontario, Ontario, Canada, with a population of approximately 55,071. Manufacturing, military (cfb trenton nearby), healthcare, agriculture, food processing (kellogg's, parmalat).
curatedFacts.longDesc:

What makes Belleville remarkable for {BUSINESS_TYPE}? Character and community. The city encompasses everything from Belleville Waterfront Trail, Bay of Quinte fishing, Quinte Mall, Empire Theatre, downtown murals. Each neighborhood has its own character, its own needs, and its own expectations.

The local economy is driven by manufacturing, military (CFB Trenton nearby), healthcare, agriculture, food processing (Kellogg's, Parmalat). This economic foundation creates steady demand for services and supports a community that takes pride in maintaining its properties and infrastructure.
